qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unvalidated binding version enables path traversal outside VP_HOME
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same public `vite-plus/binding` `downloadPackageManager()` entrypoint, same missing validation on `options.version`, and the same delete/rename/shim-write path outside `VP_HOME` described by the advisory.

selected_finding.title: Unvalidated binding version enables path traversal outside VP_HOME

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's exact root cause: the binding forwards raw `options.version` into the Rust download flow, which joins that unvalidated string into the installation path and then removes, renames, and writes shims at the attacker-influenced location. A fresh judge-side PoC run from a copied workspace showed the install directory resolve outside `VP_HOME` and created the shim there.
evidence: `crates/vite_install/src/package_manager.rs:383-412` accepts any non-`latest` string as `version` and joins it directly into `$VP_HOME/package_manager/<pm>/<version>`.
`crates/vite_install/src/package_manager.rs:471-478` removes the pre-existing target directory, renames the extracted package into the attacker-influenced target, and creates shim files there.

`/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_008a361d/.judge/poc_runs/binding_download_package_manager_path_traversal/run.log` shows `resultInstallDir` containing `../../../escaped/pnpm`, `resolvedInstallDir` resolving to `/tmp/.../escaped/pnpm` outside `vpHome`, a `shimPath` at that escaped location, and `EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC returned an install path that normalized to `/tmp/.../escaped/pnpm` outside `VP_HOME` and confirmed a created shim at `/tmp/.../escaped/pnpm/bin/pnpm`, demonstrating traversal-driven write outside the intended cache root.
